llcFilesWith: Utah Division of Corporations and Commercial Code (DBA / assumed name registration)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Utah Division of Corporations and Commercial Code (the same DBA registration)
feeBasis: The Division of Corporations charges a flat $22 to register a DBA, the same amount for an LLC or a sole proprietor. Expedited processing is an extra $75.
renewalRule: Renewed every three years from the registration date (most Utah entities renew annually, but a DBA is a three-year cycle). A DBA name cannot be amended, so changing the name means a new filing, and an expired DBA cannot be reinstated.
protectionNote: The Division states that registering a DBA does not authorize using the name in violation of another party's rights under federal, state, or common law, so it grants no exclusive right. It does sit in the records the Division screens for distinguishability, so while active a DBA blocks a later entity or DBA name that is not distinguishable from it.
searchNote: DBAs are registered with the Division of Corporations and appear in the same business search as LLCs and corporations, since they share the distinguishability records.
statuteCitation: Utah Code Title 42, Chapter 2 (Conducting Business Under Assumed Name)
notes: Utah runs a clean state-level DBA: one $22 registration with the Division of Corporations, statewide, renewable every three years, with no county filing and no publication. Registration is a disclosure filing, not a grant of rights, but it does join the Division's distinguishability records.

sources.citation: Utah Division of Corporations and Commercial Code
sources.title: DBA (registration $22, three-year term, no name protection, cannot be amended)
